number.title: The Seed of Fire
number.content: One is the beginning, the primordial spark from which all creation unfurls. It is the seed that contains the entire blueprint of the forest within its humble shell. In the Mayan cosmology, the number One represents unity, potential, and the focused initiation of a new cycle. It is not merely a starting line but a concentrated point of power where intention is most fertile. Experientially, this energy feels like a sudden clarity or an urge to plant something new—whether a project, a relationship, or a habit. The shadow of One can manifest as isolation or a lack of momentum if the seed is not watered with action. It asks you to stand firmly in your individuality while recognizing that you are the origin point of the ripple you are about to create. Today, the universe holds its breath, waiting for you to make the first move.

nawal.title: The Owl of Forgiveness
nawal.content: Ajmaq is the nawal of pardon, redemption, and the profoundly human experience of fallibility. Represented by the owl and the bee, it governs the sweetness of life that returns after we have reconciled with our errors. It is the energy of the ancestors, carrying the wisdom of the lineage and the ability to clear karmic debts through genuine acknowledgment and forgiveness. Ajmaq is not about perfection; it is about the grace found in admitting we are imperfect. It teaches us that wisdom often comes from the 'mistake,' not the success. In its shadow aspect, Ajmaq can lead to wallowing in guilt, hiding from one's actions, or repeating patterns without learning. However, when aligned, it offers the deepest form of liberation: the ability to forgive oneself and others, restoring harmony to the web of life. It is the soft darkness that holds us while we heal.

combined_energy.title: Seeding the Path of Reconciliation
combined_energy.content: The convergence of the number One with the nawal Ajmaq creates a powerful opening for self-forgiveness and a new chapter of emotional freedom. While One brings the energy of new beginnings and singular focus, Ajmaq softens this drive with the wisdom of the ancestors and the acceptance of human imperfection. This day is not about launching a business or building a physical structure; it is about initiating a new relationship with your own past. It is the 'Seed of Forgiveness.' You are being asked to look at where you have been harsh with yourself or others and to plant a seed of mercy in that wound. The energy feels tender, quiet, and profound—like the first light of dawn touching a dark forest. The challenge today is to avoid the trap of shame which might try to stifle this new beginning. Instead, use the focused power of the One to cut through the noise of guilt and declare a new standard of gentleness for your life. This is the day to set the intention that your journey forward will be lighter than the one behind you.
notes: Identify one past mistake you are ready to stop punishing yourself for.
Light a candle for your ancestors and ask for their guidance in clearing old patterns.
Write down a grievance on a piece of paper and safely burn it to symbolize release.
Practice the phrase 'I am learning' instead of 'I failed' throughout the day.
Sit in silence and visualize a seed of golden light planted in your heart center.

birthday.title: The Initiator of Grace
birthday.content:
To be born on 1 Ajmaq is to be a pioneer of forgiveness and a carrier of ancestral hope. You possess a unique soul signature that blends the initiating power of the number One with the deep, redemptive wisdom of Ajmaq. You are likely the person in your family or community who breaks the cycle—the one who decides that the trauma stops here. Your presence naturally invites others to drop their defenses and admit their vulnerabilities, for you carry an aura of non-judgmental acceptance. You are a 'first mover' in the realms of healing, often finding yourself in situations where you must lead by example in offering apology or amnesty.

Your life path is one of discovering the strength in softness. While others may see forgiveness as weakness, you understand it as a potent force of creation. You have a natural affinity for history, lineage, and the hidden stories of the past, often feeling a strong connection to those who came before you. You are a listener, a keeper of secrets, and a weaver of reconciliation. The sweetness of life is your birthright, but you must claim it by refusing to carry burdens that do not belong to you.

The challenge for you is to ensure that your capacity for forgiveness does not turn into a lack of boundaries. The number One gives you a strong sense of self, but Ajmaq's desire to harmonize can sometimes lead you to absorb the emotions of others. Your growth lies in learning that you can love and forgive without losing your own center. You are here to teach the world that every new beginning starts with making peace with the end.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ajmaq (Owl/Vulture/Bee) glowing with internal violet and honey-gold light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 1: a single glowing golden dot. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Mirror of Duality
number.content: Two is the energy of polarity, partnership, and the dynamic tension between opposites. In the Mayan worldview, nothing exists in isolation; everything has a counterpart. Two brings the awareness of the 'other'—light and dark, masculine and feminine, sun and moon. It is the number of relationships and choices. Experientially, a Two day often presents a fork in the road or a situation that requires you to weigh two sides of a coin. It is not necessarily a day of conflict, but one of comparison and reflection. The energy asks for balance and cooperation. Its shadow can manifest as indecision or seeing separation where there should be connection. It teaches us that duality is not a battle to be won, but a dance to be partnered with.

nawal.title: The Mind of the Earth
nawal.content: No'j is the nawal of wisdom, intellect, and the transmutation of knowledge into understanding. It represents the brain and the mind, but specifically the mind that is connected to the Earth—grounded, practical wisdom rather than abstract theory. No'j is the problem-solver, the planner, and the keeper of ancient science. It governs the movement of the earth (earthquakes) and the movement of the mind (ideas). In its aligned state, No'j brings brilliant clarity, innovative solutions, and a deep appreciation for learning. In its shadow, it can lead to overthinking, arrogance, or becoming trapped in one's own mental loops. No'j asks us to use our intelligence to serve the collective, turning raw data into nourishment for the community.

combined_energy.title: Harmonizing the Dual Mind
combined_energy.content: When the polarizing energy of Two meets the intellectual force of No'j, we enter a day of 'Shared Wisdom' or 'Dialoguing Minds.' The energy today focuses on the relationship between ideas—how one thought balances or challenges another. It is an excellent day for collaboration, debate, and finding the middle ground between opposing viewpoints. You may find yourself playing the role of the diplomat or the synthesizer, taking two disparate concepts and weaving them into a coherent solution. The experience is one of mental activity and relational reflection. The challenge lies in the potential for mental conflict—indecision paralysis or an argument where two 'rights' clash. However, the opportunity is to see the other side of the argument with crystal clarity. It is a day to realize that wisdom is rarely a monologue; it is a conversation between the known and the unknown, the self and the other.
notes: Engage in a conversation with someone who holds a different perspective than you.
Write down a pro/con list for a decision you have been avoiding.
Read a book or article on a subject you know nothing about to stimulate new neural pathways.
Practice active listening; listen to understand, not to reply.
If you feel mentally scattered, walk barefoot on the earth to ground your thoughts.

birthday.title: The Diplomat of Wisdom
birthday.content:
To be born on 2 No'j is to be a bridge between minds and a harmonizer of opposing truths. You carry the dual gift of high intellect and profound empathy for the 'other side.' Your mind works dialectically; you naturally see the thesis and the antithesis, and your life's work often involves finding the synthesis. You are the person friends come to when they need a fair, unbiased opinion, for you have an innate ability to detach from emotional bias and see the structural reality of a situation. You are a partner in thought, thriving in collaborations where ideas can be bounced back and forth like a game of tennis.

Your intelligence is not cold or academic; it is relational. You understand that knowledge is most useful when it connects people. You may find yourself drawn to fields like law, counseling, diplomacy, or teaching, where the goal is to bridge gaps in understanding. You are a natural problem-solver who prefers to work in tandem rather than alone. The 'Two' energy gives you a charm and approachability that softens the sometimes intense mental energy of No'j, making you a relatable and beloved teacher or guide.

The challenge for you is the burden of over-analysis. Seeing both sides of everything can sometimes leave you stuck in the middle, unable to make a firm choice for yourself. You may also struggle with doubting your own wisdom if it isn't validated by a partner. Your evolution involves learning to trust your own inner counsel as much as you value the input of others. You are here to show that true wisdom is a cooperative act.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for No'j (Brain/Thought) glowing with internal electric blue and earth-brown light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 2: two glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Rhythm of Movement
number.content: Three is the number of vibration, rhythm, and the break from duality into motion. If One is a point and Two is a line, Three is a triangle—the first stable structure, yet also an energy of dynamism. It represents the flow of communication and the spark of creativity that arises when two opposites interact. Experientially, Three feels like a surge of energy, a need to express, or a shift in the wind. It is often associated with the arts, speech, and the nervous system. The shadow of Three can be scattered energy, anxiety, or a lack of focus, like a current that is too erratic to power a machine. It asks us to move, to speak, and to let the energy flow rather than stagnate.

nawal.title: The Obsidian Blade
nawal.content: Tijax is the double-edged obsidian knife, the nawal of discrimination, healing, and cutting away what no longer serves. It is the archetype of the spiritual warrior and the surgeon. Tijax cuts to the truth, slicing through illusion, illness, and negativity. It is an intense energy that demands integrity and alignment. In the physical realm, it governs suffering and the healing of that suffering. Tijax people are often healers, doctors, or critics—those who can see exactly where the problem lies and have the courage to excise it. In its shadow, Tijax can be gossipy, harsh, or prone to accidents and conflict. However, its highest purpose is to refine reality, carving out the beautiful sculpture from the rough stone.

combined_energy.title: The Dance of Radical Truth
combined_energy.content: Today, the rhythmic motion of Three propels the sharp truth of Tijax into action. This is a day of 'Active Healing' or 'Communicating Truth.' The energy is fast-moving and decisive. It is not a day to ruminate; it is a day to cut the cord, speak the difficult truth, or make the change you have been hesitating on. The Three energy prevents the Tijax blade from staying in the sheath—it wants to move, to work, to create flow by removing blockages. You might feel a sudden urge to declutter your home, end a toxic conversation, or finally book that therapy appointment. The vibration is high and perhaps a bit jagged. The challenge is to wield this blade with rhythm and grace rather than erratic slashing. Words spoken today will be sharp and penetrate deeply, so ensure they are dipped in the medicine of healing, not just the poison of anger. It is a day to surgically remove the stagnation from your life and dance in the space that opens up.
notes: Identify a habit that drains your energy and take one concrete step to stop it today.
Have an honest conversation that you have been avoiding, but keep your tone calm.
Clean or reorganize a physical space in your home to get energy moving.
Be mindful of handling sharp objects or driving; the energy can be accident-prone.
Visualize cutting energetic cords that attach you to past negativity.

birthday.title: The Eloquent Healer
birthday.content:
To be born on 3 Tijax is to be a voice for truth and an agent of rapid transformation. You possess a mind that is quick, sharp, and incisive, coupled with a communicative flair that makes you hard to ignore. You are the person who says what everyone else is thinking but is afraid to voice. Your words are your scalpel, and you have the potential to be a brilliant writer, speaker, or advocate who exposes injustice and promotes healing. The number Three gives you a restlessness and a need for variety; you are likely multitalented and easily bored by routine. You move through life with a certain velocity, often bringing change wherever you go.

Your healing capacity comes from your ability to diagnose the root cause of an issue instantly. Whether it's a structural problem in a business or an emotional blockage in a friend, you see the crack in the foundation. You are not one to sugarcoat reality, and while this honesty is your greatest gift, it can also be your social stumbling block. You are a 'truth-cutter' who dances with the blade, finding a strange kind of joy in the precision of reality.

The challenge for you lies in the management of your own nervous energy and the sharpness of your tongue. You may be prone to anxiety or impulsive speech that wounds rather than heals. Your life lesson is to learn the timing of the surgeon—knowing exactly when to cut and when to bandage. When you master your own intensity, you become a powerful force for liberation, using your voice to set others free from their illusions.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Tijax (Obsidian Knife/Pyramid) glowing with internal metallic silver and volcanic red light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 3: three glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Cube of Stability
number.content: Four is the number of stability, form, and the physical plane. It represents the four directions (East, West, North, South), the four elements, and the four pillars that hold up the sky. After the movement of Three, Four brings things back to earth to be grounded and tested. It is the energy of definition, boundaries, and foundation. Experientially, a Four day feels solid, reliable, and practical. It is a time to work, to build, and to organize. The shadow of Four can be rigidity, stubbornness, or a feeling of being 'boxed in.' It asks us to create a container for our spirit, ensuring that our dreams have a sturdy place to land.

nawal.title: The Storm of Compassion
nawal.content: Kawoq is the energy of the storm, the midwife, and the community. It represents the gathering of clouds, the thunder that precedes the rain, and the breaking of waters at birth. Kawoq is a feminine energy, powerful and cleansing, focused on the well-being of the collective and the family. It is the force that pushes us through the birth canal of transformation. While it can be turbulent, its intent is always to bring new life and abundance. Kawoq governs the home, the hearth, and the circle of community. In its shadow, it can manifest as emotional overwhelm, intrusiveness, or the chaos of a storm that destroys rather than waters. Its highest expression is the compassionate warmth of the heated stones in the sweat lodge.

combined_energy.title: Building the Sanctuary
combined_energy.content: When the structural stability of Four meets the compassionate storm of Kawoq, the result is an energy of 'Protected Transformation' or 'Sanctuary Building.' Today is about creating a safe, solid container for feelings, family, and community. It is the perfect day to work on your home, not just physically, but energetically—making it a fortress of peace. The Four stabilizes the volatility of the Kawoq storm, turning a potential flood into a well-managed irrigation system. This combination favors practical acts of care: cooking a meal for loved ones, fixing a leak, or organizing community resources. It suggests that true abundance requires a strong vessel to hold it. The challenge is to balance the need for order with the flow of emotion; do not build walls so thick that they keep love out. Instead, build a hearth where the fire can burn safely. Trust that the structures you reinforce today will support the new life that is trying to be born.
notes: Clean and declutter your living space to honor the energy of the home.
Host a small gathering or dinner for close family or friends.
Check on the 'foundations' of your life—finances, health, basic needs.
Practice patience; allow processes to unfold within their own safe container.
Visualize a golden cube of light protecting your home and loved ones.

birthday.title: The Guardian of the Hearth
birthday.content:
To be born on 4 Kawoq is to be a pillar of the community and a builder of safe havens. You possess a nurturing spirit that is grounded in practical action. You are likely the 'rock' of your family, the one who provides both the shoulder to cry on and the roof over everyone's head. The number Four gives you a deep love for stability, tradition, and order, while Kawoq fills you with empathy and a drive to care for the collective. You are not a solitary creature; you thrive when you are responsible for the well-being of a group, whether that is a large family, a team, or a neighborhood.

Your approach to life is maternal (regardless of gender) and architectural. You want to build systems of care that last. You might be drawn to professions in social work, housing, nursing, or community planning. You understand that love is not just a feeling, but a structure—it requires reliable presence and tangible support. People feel safe around you because you exude a sense of permanence and protection. You are the calm eye within the storm, capable of holding space for intense emotions without being toppled by them.

The challenge for you is the tendency to over-give or to become rigid in your role as the protector. You may struggle to let others take care of themselves, feeling that the roof will collapse if you aren't holding it up. Your lesson is to build structures that are flexible enough to allow for growth and to remember that you, too, deserve a place of sanctuary. You are here to demonstrate that the strongest foundation is one built on compassion.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kawoq (Storm Clouds/Turtle) glowing with internal teal and electric purple light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 4: four glowing golden dots arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Hand of Action
number.content: Five is the number of empowerment, work, and the human hand (five fingers). It represents the energy of doing—gathering resources, applying effort, and making things happen in the material world. After the stability of Four, Five sets things in motion again through work and will. It is often associated with the 'center' or the core that drives the four directions. Experientially, a Five day asks for participation. It is not a day to sit on the sidelines. It carries a net-gathering quality, pulling in what is needed. The shadow of Five can be overworking, forcing outcomes, or losing the spiritual connection in the busyness of the physical. It reminds us that we have the power to shape our reality through our actions.

nawal.title: The Solar Hero
nawal.content: Ajpu is the nawal of the Sun, the blowgunner, and the spiritual warrior. It represents the light of divinity within the human form. Ajpu is the archetype of the hero who descends into the underworld (Xibalba) to overcome darkness and rise again as the sun. It governs art, performance, leadership, and the clarity of purpose. Ajpu energy is confident, radiant, and resilient. It is the hunter who aims true. In its shadow, Ajpu can manifest as ego, arrogance, or a need for validation. It can also be judgmental or overly perfectionistic. However, at its core, it is the energy of Christ-consciousness or Buddha-nature—the realization that we are light walking on earth.

combined_energy.title: The Work of Illumination
combined_energy.content: Today, the industrious energy of Five combines with the solar radiance of Ajpu to create a day of 'Practical Divinity' or 'Empowered Purpose.' This is not a day to just dream about your higher self; it is a day to *act* like it. The energy supports doing the work that aligns with your soul's mission. Whether that means practicing an art form, leading a team, or standing up for a value you believe in, the universe is handing you the tools to make it manifest. Five Ajpu is the 'working artist' or the 'servant leader.' It reminds us that spiritual light is not just a concept, but a practice. You might feel a strong drive to finish a project or to polish a skill. The challenge is to ensure your ego doesn't hijack the effort—remember that you are the vessel, not the source, of the light. Aim your blowgun with steady hands, put in the effort, and trust that your work is clearing the path for the sun to rise.
notes: Dedicate one hour of focused work to a creative passion or hobby.
Stand in the sunlight for a few minutes and visualize recharging your solar plexus.
Do a physical act of service that aligns with your personal values.
Practice confidence; speak up in a meeting or share your work with others.
Reflect on what 'heroism' looks like in your daily life.

birthday.title: The Radiant Worker
birthday.content:
To be born on 5 Ajpu is to be a beacon of practical light and a tireless champion for the divine. You combine the work ethic of the number Five with the visionary leadership of the Sun. You are not content to simply have a vision; you are compelled to build it, brick by brick. You are likely a natural leader who leads by example—you wouldn't ask anyone to do a task you aren't willing to do yourself. People are drawn to your confidence and your capability; you have a 'can-do' aura that makes difficult feats seem possible. You often find yourself in roles where you must perform or present, and you shine in the spotlight, but always with a sense of duty behind it.

Your life path involves mastering the art of manifestation. You are a hunter of dreams, using your focus and determination to bring spiritual ideals down into physical reality. You may be drawn to the arts, athletics, or advocacy, any field where discipline meets expression. You have a strong sense of justice and a warrior's spirit when it comes to defending the weak. However, your battle is often internal—the quest to refine your own character until it is pure gold.

The challenge for you is the potential for burnout or perfectionism. The Five energy drives you to keep working, while Ajpu demands the highest standard. You may struggle to rest or to accept 'good enough.' Your evolution lies in realizing that you are already worthy, not because of what you do, but because of who you are. You are here to show the world that the holiest act is often the simple, daily labor of love.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Ajpu (Sun Face/Blowgunner) glowing with internal golden-white and amber light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 5: a single glowing golden bar. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Flow of Harmony
number.content: Six is the number of flow, responsiveness, and organic stability. It represents the point where the initial effort stabilizes and begins to find its rhythm. In Mayan cosmology, Six is often associated with the Heart of the Sky and the Heart of the Earth connecting. It is a number of family, physical vitality, and the natural flow of life events. Experientially, a Six day feels smoother than the previous days; obstacles tend to dissolve if you stop pushing and start flowing. It is a time for acceptance and alignment. The shadow of Six can be passivity or a lack of direction, drifting too much with the current. It teaches us to trust the process and move with the Tao of the moment.

nawal.title: The Dream of the Waters
nawal.content: Imox is the nawal of the primordial ocean, the collective unconscious, and the dream world. It represents the water that connects all things—the rain, the rivers, the blood in our veins. Imox is the source of all creativity, but it is also the realm of madness and illusion. It is the energy of the 'crazies,' the visionaries, and the empaths. Imox governs the psyche and the hidden currents of emotion. In its aligned state, it is the fountain of artistic genius and psychic intuition. In its shadow, it can be a drowning pool of confusion, paranoia, or emotional instability. It asks us to navigate the depths without losing our breath.

combined_energy.title: Navigating the Emotional Tide
combined_energy.content: As the harmonious flow of Six meets the watery depths of Imox, we enter a day of 'Fluid Intuition' or 'Emotional Flow.' The energy today is incredibly psychic and permeable. The boundaries between you and the world, or between reality and dream, may feel thin. This is a beautiful energy for artists, healers, and lovers, as it allows for a deep, wordless connection. The Six helps to stabilize the potentially chaotic waters of Imox, making this a safer day than usual to explore your subconscious. You may feel a strong pull toward water, music, or meditation. The challenge is to not get swept away by the collective emotional tide. You might pick up on feelings that aren't yours. The key is to let them flow *through* you, not get stuck *in* you. Trust your gut feelings today; your logic may be foggy, but your intuition is flowing like a clear river.
notes: Spend time near a body of water, or take a long mindful bath.
Keep a dream journal next to your bed; messages are likely to come in sleep.
Engage in free-flow creative activities like painting or dancing without a plan.
Practice energetic shielding if you are in crowded places.
Drink plenty of water to honor the element of the day.

birthday.title: The Intuitive Flow
birthday.content:
To be born on 6 Imox is to be a vessel for the collective dream and a master of emotional currents. You possess a soul that is deeply connected to the invisible realms. You are likely an empath, feeling the moods of a room the moment you walk in. The number Six gives you a natural grace and an ability to navigate these waters with relative stability, preventing you from drowning in the depths that might overwhelm others. You are a natural healer or therapist, intuitively knowing what others need before they speak it. Your creativity is boundless, often springing from a source you can't explain—you are a channel for the muse.

Your life path is one of bringing the formless into form. You may struggle with the rigid structures of the 'real world'—schedules, deadlines, and dry logic are foreign languages to you. You thrive in environments that allow for fluidity and feeling. You are the friend who listens without judgment, the artist who paints the soul, the dreamer who reminds us that reality is malleable. People are drawn to your soothing, watery presence.

The challenge for you is maintaining your own boundaries. Because you are so porous, you can easily become a psychic sponge for the world's pain. You may also struggle with 'Imox madness'—a sense of confusion or ungroundedness when you lose touch with the shore. Your lesson is to learn to be the riverbed as well as the water—to contain your own flow so that it can be directed toward healing and creation rather than dissipation. You are here to teach the world to trust the unseen.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Imox (Water/Crocodile/Chalice) glowing with internal deep turquoise and sea-foam green light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 6: one glowing golden bar with one glowing dot above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Summit of Vision
number.content: Seven is the number of the midpoint, the top of the pyramid, and the power of mystical alignment. Sitting exactly in the middle of the 1 to 13 cycle, Seven represents the ability to see both the past (1-6) and the future (8-13) simultaneously. It is a number of reflection, choice, and equilibrium. Unlike the static balance of Two or Four, Seven is a dynamic, spiritual balance. It is often a day where energy peaks or shifts. Experientially, a Seven day feels like reaching a lookout point—there is clarity, perspective, and a sense of 'anything is possible.' The shadow of Seven can be indecision born of seeing too many options or a detachment from the ground. It asks us to look at the whole picture before taking the next step.

nawal.title: The Breath of Spirit
nawal.content: Iq' is the nawal of the wind, the breath, and communication. It represents the spirit (Ruach/Prana) that animates all living things. Iq' is the messenger of the gods, carrying prayers up to the sky and inspiration down to the earth. It governs the respiratory system and the mind's agility. Iq' energy is quick, changeable, and purifying. It brings the winds of change that sweep away the old to make space for the new. In its shadow, Iq' can be fickle, angry (hurricanes), or prone to gossip and empty words. Its highest expression is the clear, inspired voice of the poet or the prophet who speaks truth from the spirit.

combined_energy.title: The Voice of the Summit
combined_energy.content: When the visionary peak of Seven combines with the communicative wind of Iq', we experience a day of 'Higher Inspiration' or 'Spiritual Perspective.' The energy is airy, expansive, and electric. It is one of the best days in the calendar for seeing the 'big picture' of your life and understanding the messages the universe has been trying to send you. The wind blows freely at the top of the pyramid, clearing away mental fog. You may feel a sudden shift in perspective regarding a problem that has plagued you, or receive a flash of insight that connects the dots of your past and future. It is a powerful day for prayer, meditation, and setting intentions, as the channel between earth and sky is wide open. The challenge is to stay grounded enough to catch these messages without getting lost in the ether. The wind can be erratic; use the Seven's balance to center yourself in the eye of the storm. Breathe deeply, listen to the silence between thoughts, and let the spirit speak.
notes: Practice conscious breathwork or pranayama to align with the wind.
Go to a high place (a hill, a rooftop, a balcony) to physically embody the perspective.
Write a letter to your future self or to the universe.
Ventilate your home; open windows to let fresh air and energy circulate.
Listen to wind chimes or the sound of the breeze to clear your mind.

birthday.title: The Visionary Messenger
birthday.content:
To be born on 7 Iq' is to be a conduit for higher truth and a catalyst for change. You stand at the crossroads of the seen and unseen, possessed of a mind that is constantly synthesizing information from both worlds. The number Seven gives you a mystical, reflective nature—you are a seeker who is always looking for the deeper meaning. Combined with the wind of Iq', this makes you a powerful communicator of spiritual or visionary concepts. You may be a writer, a philosopher, a traveler, or a teacher who brings 'fresh air' into stale systems. You have a knack for seeing the trajectory of things, knowing which way the wind is blowing before anyone else does.

Your life is likely marked by periods of rapid change and movement. You are not meant to stay stagnant; your soul requires freedom and circulation. You are the person who brings the news, the new idea, or the necessary disruption that clears the air. People look to you for perspective because you seem to float a little above the fray, able to see the layout of the land with detachment and clarity.

The challenge for you is consistency. Like the wind, you can be unpredictable, here one moment and gone the next. The Seven energy can also lead you to over-intellectualize or detach from emotional realities. Your growth involves learning to land your ideas—to not just be the wind that blows through, but the breath that sustains. You are here to remind the world that spirit is as real as the air we breathe, and that change is the only constant.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Iq' (Wind/T-Shape) glowing with internal crystalline white and pale blue light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 7: one glowing golden bar with two glowing dots above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Weaver of Harmony
number.content: Eight is the number of wholeness, justice, and the octave. In Mayan tradition, it represents the masculine energy (related to the father) and the fabric of the weave. It is considered a lucky number, representing the completion of a harmonic cycle—like the notes in a musical scale returning to the tonic. Eight brings the energy of organization, return, and infinite flow. Experientially, an Eight day feels balanced, potent, and ceremonial. It is the traditional number for day-keeping rituals. The shadow of Eight can be rigidity or a feeling of being caught in a loop if the flow is not maintained. It asks us to weave the threads of our lives into a coherent pattern.

nawal.title: The Dawn of Possibility
nawal.content: Aq'ab'al is the nawal of the dawn, the twilight, and the threshold. It represents the moment when night turns to day (and vice versa)—the mixing of light and shadow. Aq'ab'al is the energy of new beginnings, potential, and the realization of hope. It governs the polarity of young and old, darkness and light. It is a romantic, optimistic energy that sees the beauty in transition. In its shadow, Aq'ab'al can manifest as duality, deception, or the inability to fully emerge from the dark—staying in the 'half-light' of ambiguity. Its highest purpose is to bring the light of clarity to the darkness of confusion, acting as the harbinger of a new day.

combined_energy.title: Harmonizing the New Light
combined_energy.content: Today, the harmonic wholeness of Eight meets the hopeful dawn of Aq'ab'al, creating a day of 'Balanced Beginnings' or 'Ceremonial Renewal.' This is a very auspicious day. The Eight provides a stable, ceremonial container for the new potentials that Aq'ab'al brings. It is a day where the changes you are implementing (or the new chapters you are starting) can be integrated smoothly into the fabric of your life. The 'wildness' of the new is tamed by the harmony of the Eight. It is an excellent day for marriage, signing contracts, or initiating a long-term project. The energy feels like a sunrise witnessed from a perfectly built temple—beautiful, orderly, and sacred. The challenge is to ensure you are honoring both the light and the shadow. Aq'ab'al reminds us that every dawn leaves a night behind. Use the justice of the Eight to give thanks for what is ending as you welcome what is beginning. Weave the polarity into a single, strong cloth.
notes: Wake up early to watch the sunrise and set a specific intention for the cycle.
Organize your calendar or schedule to support a new habit.
Perform a small ritual of gratitude (lighting a candle) for the 'dark' times that taught you lessons.
Look for 'signs' or coincidences today; the veil is thin at the threshold.
Wear colors that represent dawn (orange, pink, grey) to align with the energy.

birthday.title: The Harmonizer of Transitions
birthday.content:
To be born on 8 Aq'ab'al is to be a weaver of hope and a master of timing. You carry the energy of the eternal dawn—a perpetual freshness and optimism that inspires those around you. The number Eight gives you a strong sense of structure, justice, and community standing, while Aq'ab'al fills you with the creative potential of the new day. You are the person who can walk into a chaotic or dark situation and see exactly how to bring the light in. You don't just hope for a better future; you have the organizational skills to build it.

Your life path is one of bridging worlds. You are comfortable in the liminal spaces where others feel lost. You may find yourself working in fields that involve transition—hospice, midwifery, career counseling, or renovation—helping people move from one state of being to another. You have a romantic soul and an artist's eye for contrast. You understand that light is defined by shadow, and you are not afraid of the dark corners of the human experience.

The challenge for you is indecision or getting stuck in the 'in-between.' You may have a tendency to keep your options open for too long, loving the potential of the dawn so much that you hesitate to step into the full light of day. Your lesson is to commit—to choose a path and weave it into reality. When you do, you become a powerful agent of renewal, showing the world that every ending is simply the raw material for a beautiful beginning.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Aq'ab'al (Bat/Dawn) glowing with internal sunrise-orange and twilight-indigo light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 8: one glowing golden bar with three glowing dots above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Circle of Gestation
number.content: Nine is the number of the feminine, patience, and the completion of cycles (associated with the 9 months of pregnancy). It represents the deep, subterranean work that happens before manifestation. Nine is emotional, intuitive, and connected to the mysteries of life and death. Experientially, a Nine day feels slower, deeper, and more internal. It is not a day for quick results, but for nurturing what is growing in the dark. The shadow of Nine can be moodiness, withdrawal, or getting stuck in the past. It asks us to trust the timing of the universe and to care for the seeds we have planted.

nawal.title: The Net of Abundance
nawal.content: K'at is the nawal of the net, the web, and the gathering. It represents the lizard (the seed-carrier) and the energy of abundance that comes from connection. K'at is the force that pulls things together—people, resources, ideas. It governs the harvest and the storage of grain. However, the net can also trap. K'at also represents our entanglements—debts, burdens, and emotional baggage that we carry in our 'net.' In its shadow, K'at can manifest as hoarding, feeling trapped by obligations, or being unable to let go. Its highest purpose is to gather what nurtures us and release what weighs us down, keeping our net light and full of blessings.

combined_energy.title: Gestating Freedom
combined_energy.content: The feminine depth of Nine meets the gathering power of K'at, creating a day of 'Deep Sorting' or 'Nurtured Release.' Today asks you to look into your net—your subconscious, your emotional baggage, your stored memories—and see what is truly worth carrying. The Nine energy allows you to do this with patience and compassion, without the rush to just 'fix it.' It is a day of emotional accounting. You might feel the weight of old stories more heavily today, but this is so you can acknowledge them and prepare to set them down. It is also a powerful day for fertility and creativity; the 'net' is full of potential life waiting to be born. The challenge is the risk of becoming overwhelmed by how much you are carrying. If you feel heavy, remember that the purpose of the net is to hold the harvest, not the trash. Use the gentle, gestation energy of Nine to slowly untangle the knots. Nurture the connections that feed your soul and let the rest slip through the mesh.
notes: Visualize a net of golden light and mentally place your worries into it to be transmuted.
Pay a debt (financial or energetic) to clear your 'net.'
Spend time organizing a collection or a storage space in your home.
Connect with a female mentor or mother figure.
Reflect on what you are 'pregnant' with—what project or feeling is almost ready to be born?

birthday.title: The Keeper of the Harvest
birthday.content:
To be born on 9 K'at is to be a soul of deep abundance and complex emotional richness. You are a gatherer of life's experiences, carrying a net that is overflowing with stories, connections, and wisdom. The number Nine gives you a profound feminine intuition and a patient, nurturing nature. You understand that good things take time. K'at makes you a connector—you likely have a vast network of friends and resources, and you love to bring people together. You are the 'glue' in your community, the one who holds the collective memory.

Your life path involves learning the art of release. Because you are such a powerful attractor and container, you can easily become weighed down by the burdens of others or by your own past. You may struggle with letting go—whether of possessions, relationships, or old hurts. Your journey is to learn that your net is magical; it will always refill. You do not need to hoard. You are a natural psychologist or archivist, able to sort through the clutter of the human psyche and find the gems hidden therein.

The challenge for you is avoiding the trap of your own making. You can become entangled in drama or obligations because you have a hard time saying no. Your lesson is to keep your net clean and repaired. When you master this, you become a source of infinite nourishment for the world, holding space for everyone without ever being trapped by anyone.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for K'at (Net/Lizard) glowing with internal harvest-gold and earthy-green light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 9: one glowing golden bar with four glowing dots above it. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Hand of Manifestation
number.content: Ten is the number of manifestation, responsibility, and the two hands clasping together. It represents the realization of the cycle—where the intention of One and the work of Five come to fruition. Ten is a number of community agreement, law, and the tangible results of our efforts. Experientially, a Ten day feels solid, accomplished, and sometimes heavy with expectation. It is a day where things 'land.' The shadow of Ten can be the burden of responsibility or the harshness of judgment if the results are not what was hoped for. It asks us to own our creations and stand by what we have built.

nawal.title: The Serpent of Wisdom
nawal.content: Kan is the nawal of the serpent, the life force (kundalini), and evolution. It represents the inner fire that coils at the base of the spine and rises to bring enlightenment. Kan is the energy of DNA, the spiral, and the power of transformation through shedding skin. It governs sexuality, vitality, and justice. Kan is an intense, instinctual energy—it is the raw power of nature. In its shadow, Kan can be manipulative, seductive in a negative way, or prone to misuse of power. It represents the test of integrity. Its highest expression is the wise use of power to serve the evolution of consciousness, moving with the fluidity and strength of the snake.

combined_energy.title: Manifesting Vitality
combined_energy.content: When the manifesting power of Ten combines with the evolutionary force of Kan, we have a day of 'Realized Power' or 'Materialized Wisdom.' This is a high-voltage day. The abstract potential of the serpent's energy is being grounded into tangible form by the Ten. It is an incredible day for physical healing, athletic pursuits, or taking a leadership role that requires charisma and strength. Things you have been working on may suddenly shed their old skins and reveal their true, shiny forms. The energy is authoritative and demands respect. The challenge is to handle this power with integrity. Ten Kan can be overwhelming if your nervous system isn't ready for the voltage. It can also tempt you to use your influence for control rather than service. The lesson is to be the 'wise container' for the energy—to let the power flow through you into the world, creating structures (Ten) that support life and growth (Kan).
notes: Engage in physical exercise like yoga or dance to move the energy in your spine.
Take ownership of a situation where you have given your power away.
Focus on 'grounding' your ideas; what is the practical step to make the vision real?
Be mindful of your lower back and spine; stretch and breathe into these areas.
Visualize a caduceus (staff with two snakes) representing your balanced power.

birthday.title: The Master of Life Force
birthday.content:
To be born on 10 Kan is to be a powerhouse of energy and a natural leader of evolution. You possess a magnetism that is palpable; people are drawn to your vitality and your strength. The number Ten gives you a sense of authority and a drive to build tangible results, while Kan endows you with deep instinctual wisdom and a connection to the primal forces of life. You are likely very physical, intelligent, and possibly gifted in the healing arts or in fields that require high stamina and strategy. You are a 'manifestor'—when you put your mind to something, you have the sheer force of will to make it happen.

Your life path is one of mastering your own power. You have a Ferrari engine, and you need to learn to drive it without crashing. You may face lessons around the use of influence, sexuality, or material wealth. You are a teacher of transformation, often by modeling it yourself—shedding skins publicly and emerging stronger each time. You have a deep sense of justice and will fight fiercely for what you believe is right.

The challenge for you is not to be consumed by your own fire. You can be intense, and sometimes this intensity can burn bridges or intimidate others. Your lesson is to channel your massive energy into service and to cultivate a 'cool head' to balance your 'hot blood.' When you align your will with higher wisdom, you become a force of nature that evolves not just yourself, but everyone around you.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kan (Serpent) glowing with internal emerald-green and magma-red light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 10: two glowing golden bars arranged horizontally. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Force of Dissolution
number.content: Eleven is the number of dissonance, change, and the breaking of patterns. It is often considered a 'master number' but in the sense of a challenge—it is the energy that disrupts the status quo to clear the way for higher order. Eleven is not about stability; it is about testing the structure. Experientially, an Eleven day can feel chaotic, surprising, or liberating. It is the loose brick in the wall that, when pulled, changes the whole shape of the room. The shadow of Eleven is overwhelm or destruction without purpose. It asks us to be flexible and to find the freedom in letting things fall apart.

nawal.title: The Transformer
nawal.content: Kame is the nawal of death, rebirth, and the ancestors. It represents the dimensional door between worlds. Kame is the only certainty in life—change. It governs the cycles of ending and beginning, the compost that feeds the flower. Kame is a peaceful, quiet energy, like the silence of the grave, but it commands total respect. It is the patron of spiritual mediums and those who help souls cross over. In its shadow, Kame can be fear of change, morbidness, or clinging to the past. Its highest expression is the profound peace of surrender—knowing that death is simply a transformation of energy, an undressing of the soul.

combined_energy.title: The Great Release
combined_energy.content: Today, the disruptive force of Eleven meets the transformative power of Kame. This is a day of 'Radical Clearing' or 'The Great Let-Go.' It is perhaps the most powerful day in the trecena for breaking addictions, ending outdated contracts, or finally grieving a loss. The energy is stripping away the non-essential. It might feel intense or turbulent, as Eleven shakes the foundations while Kame dissolves the form. Do not try to hold on tightly to anything today; the grip will only cause friction. Instead, surrender to the process of shedding. This is a day to trust that what is leaving your life is making space for something much more alive. It is a 'phoenix' energy—the fire must burn before the bird can rise. The challenge is the fear of the void. But if you can sit in that emptiness without panicking, you will find a profound sense of liberation. You are being unburdened.
notes: Clean out a closet, drawer, or digital folder—delete what is dead.
Write a letter to someone or something you are ready to say goodbye to, then burn it.
Practice 'corpse pose' (Savasana) in yoga; consciously relax every muscle into the earth.
Avoid starting new major projects today; focus on closing loops.
Remind yourself: 'I am not what I lose; I am what remains.'

birthday.title: The Agent of Transformation
birthday.content:
To be born on 11 Kame is to be a catalyst for profound change and a walker between worlds. You carry a powerful, somewhat mysterious energy. The number Eleven makes you a non-conformist, someone who is here to shake up the system, while Kame gives you a deep comfort with the cycles of life and death. You are not afraid of endings; in fact, you are likely the person who helps others navigate them. You may be drawn to psychology, hospice work, shamanism, or crisis management—any field where you help people through their darkest nights.

Your life path is a series of ego-deaths and rebirths. You reinvent yourself many times, shedding identities like old clothes. This can be disorienting for those around you, but for you, it is essential survival. You have a direct line to the ancestors and may possess strong mediumistic or intuitive abilities. You see the fragility of life, which gives you a unique, often dark, sense of humor and a desire to live authentically.

The challenge for you is stability. Your life can feel like a constant earthquake if you don't find a spiritual center. You may also struggle with a sense of fatalism or heaviness. Your lesson is to learn that while everything changes, the spirit is eternal. You are here to teach the world the art of surrender and to show that on the other side of every death, there is a new, brighter life waiting.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kame (Skull/Closed Eyes) glowing with internal bone-white and soft violet light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 11: two glowing golden bars with one glowing dot above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Crystal of Understanding
number.content: Twelve is the number of understanding, summation, and the gathering of scattered parts into a whole. It represents the family, the tribe, and the complex stability that comes after the disruption of Eleven. Twelve is the number of the 'council'—it holds space for all previous experiences to be integrated. Experientially, a Twelve day feels rich, cooperative, and reflective. It is a time to look back at the journey and see the lesson. The shadow of Twelve can be heaviness or the difficulty of managing too many moving parts. It asks us to find the unity in diversity.

nawal.title: The Stag of the Wild
nawal.content: Kej is the nawal of the deer, the four directions, and the natural world. It represents the balance between the wild and the tame. Kej is the guardian of the forest and the conductor of natural harmony. It governs strength, agility, and the authority of the shaman. Kej energy is noble, strong, and sensitive. It stands with one foot in the physical world and one in the spiritual. In its shadow, Kej can be dominating, manipulative, or overly stubborn. It can also be skittish or fearful. Its highest expression is the graceful leadership that respects the laws of nature and protects the sanctity of the earth.

combined_energy.title: Integrating Natural Law
combined_energy.content: When the integrative understanding of Twelve meets the natural strength of Kej, we have a day of 'Holistic Balance' or 'The Council of Nature.' The energy today is stable, strong, and wise. It is a day to understand your place in the larger ecosystem—of your family, your community, and the planet. Twelve Kej asks you to bring all the parts of yourself into harmony with the natural order. It is an excellent day for group activities, spending time in nature, or resolving conflicts through a 'council' approach where everyone is heard. You may feel a sense of sturdy calm, like an old tree. The disruption of yesterday (11 Kame) has settled into a new, stronger landscape. The challenge is to not be too rigid or dominant in your quest for order. Let the 'wildness' of the deer soften the structure of the Twelve. Lead with grace, not force. Remember that true strength comes from balance, not from overpowering the opposition.
notes: Spend time in a forest or park; listen to the 'council' of the trees.
Hold a family meeting or team check-in to ensure everyone is aligned.
Physically ground yourself: stand on the earth, do squats, or hug a tree.
Reflect on the 'four directions' of your life: Work, Home, Spirit, Self.
Eat simple, natural foods today to honor the deer's connection to the earth.

birthday.title: The Noble Guardian
birthday.content:
To be born on 12 Kej is to be a pillar of strength and a guardian of natural wisdom. You possess a regal, dignified presence that commands respect without you having to demand it. The number Twelve gives you a deep understanding of community dynamics and a desire for social cohesion, while Kej connects you to the raw power and balance of nature. You are likely a leader in your field, someone who can manage complex systems while keeping a 'human' touch. You have a shamanic quality—an ability to read the winds and understand the unspoken laws of a situation.

Your life path is one of service through leadership. You are the 'chief' archetype, responsible for the well-being of the tribe. You may be drawn to environmental work, politics, coaching, or any role where you guide others toward balance. You are strong, but you are also sensitive; like the deer, you are always alert to the subtle shifts in your environment.

The challenge for you is the weight of the crown. You can be prone to taking on too much responsibility or becoming authoritarian when you feel out of control. Your lesson is to trust the strength of others and to remember that true leadership involves vulnerability. You are here to remind us that we are part of nature, not separate from it, and to model a strength that is as graceful as it is powerful.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Kej (Deer/Hand) glowing with internal forest-green and earth-brown light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 12: two glowing golden bars with two glowing dots above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.

number.title: The Light of Ascension
number.content: Thirteen is the number of completion, ascension, and the spirit world. It is the final step in the trecena cycle, representing the return to the source but at a higher level of spiral. Thirteen is the number of the ancestors and the 'invisible army' of support. Experientially, a Thirteen day feels mystical, thin-veiled, and sometimes surreal. It is a day of wrapping up, letting go, and preparing for the new seed (which comes tomorrow). The shadow of Thirteen can be ungroundedness or a feeling of being 'checked out' of reality. It asks us to celebrate the journey and harvest the wisdom.

nawal.title: The Golden Seed
nawal.content: Q'anil is the nawal of the seed, the rabbit, and the star. It represents the spark of life, fertility, and the potential for organic growth. Q'anil is the energy of the planter and the harvester. It is a joyful, abundant energy that governs biological life, agriculture, and artistic creation. Q'anil is the promise that 'what you plant, you will harvest.' In its shadow, Q'anil can manifest as impatience, problems with fertility/creativity, or a scattering of seeds on barren ground. Its highest expression is the blooming garden—the realization of potential into beautiful, living form.

combined_energy.title: The Harvest of Spirit
combined_energy.content: We reach the end of the Ajmaq trecena with 13 Q'anil—the 'Ascended Seed' or the 'Spiritual Harvest.' This is a beautiful, light-filled conclusion. The journey of forgiveness and reconciliation (Ajmaq) has led us here: to a place where we can plant a new, golden seed for the future. The Thirteen elevates the Q'anil energy, suggesting that what we are harvesting today is not just material, but spiritual abundance. It is a day of celebration, gratitude, and looking forward. The heavy lifting of the trecena is done. You may feel a sense of lightness, a 'cosmic wink,' or a deep appreciation for the beauty of life. Any seeds planted today (intentions, projects, relationships) are blessed with the highest frequency of the calendar. They carry the wisdom of the ancestors and the spark of the star. The challenge is simply to receive it. Do not rush to the next thing yet; pause to enjoy the fruit. This is the moment the flower opens fully to the sun.
notes: Celebrate! Do something purely for joy—eat a good meal, dance, or play.
Plant a physical seed or flower as a symbol of your intention for the next cycle.
Write a list of 'harvests' (wins/lessons) from the last 13 days.
Offer a toast to your ancestors, thanking them for guiding you through the Ajmaq work.
Visualize your heart as a golden star radiating light in all directions.

birthday.title: The Star of Destiny
birthday.content:
To be born on 13 Q'anil is to be a cosmic gardener and a carrier of the golden light. You are a 'star seed' in the truest sense—someone who brings a high, refined frequency to the earth. The number Thirteen connects you directly to the spirit world and the ancestors, giving you a powerful intuition and a sense of divine protection. Q'anil makes you a creator, a lover of life, and a natural attracter of abundance. You are the person who always seems to land on their feet, the one who finds the four-leaf clover. Your presence brings joy and growth to those around you.

Your life path is one of bringing heaven to earth. You are here to plant seeds of consciousness, beauty, and art. You may be drawn to agriculture, the arts, children, or spiritual teaching. You have a natural innocence and optimism that is rare, but it is backed by the ancient wisdom of the Thirteen. You understand that life is a cycle of planting and harvesting, and you are patient with the process.

The challenge for you is remaining grounded. With such high energy, you can sometimes float away into fantasy or spiritual bypassing. You may also struggle with the harshness of the world, as your soul is tuned to a frequency of harmony. Your lesson is to keep your hands in the dirt—to ensure that your beautiful visions are rooted in practical reality. You are here to show us that the Garden of Eden is not a place we left, but a place we can grow right here, right now.
birthday.image_prompt: Square image. A mystical stone carving of the Mayan glyph for Q'anil (Rabbit/Star/Seed) glowing with internal harvest-gold and star-white light against a dark obsidian background. Hovering directly above the glyph is the Mayan numeral 13: two glowing golden bars with three glowing dots above them. The style is ancient, tactile, and magical, emphasizing the texture of the stone and the luminescence of the symbols. No English text.
